expectations.text: Identifies the cartesian/row-explosion risk from the 1:N joins (order_item and payment) and suggests separate queries or aggregation
expectations.evidence: Finding 2: 8x2=16 near-identical rows; fix = page orders then batch-load children via ANY($1), or aggregate with LATERAL

expectations.text: Flags DATE(o.created_at) as a function/cast on the column that disables the index, and rewrites it as a range on the raw column
expectations.evidence: Finding 1: DATE() forces per-row compute, index unusable; rewrite created_at >= '2025-01-01 00:00:00'

expectations.text: Flags SELECT * and recommends selecting only the needed columns
expectations.evidence: Finding 5: SELECT * across four tables, blocks index-only scan; use explicit column list

expectations.text: Flags the high OFFSET (80000) and recommends keyset/cursor pagination
expectations.evidence: Finding 3: OFFSET 80000 reads+discards 80k rows; keyset on (created_at,id)

expectations.text: States a tradeoff for at least one recommendation (not just the fix)
expectations.evidence: Every finding has explicit Tradeoff; e.g. keyset can't jump to arbitrary page; index taxes writes

expectations.text: Provides at least one corrected SQL example
expectations.evidence: Multiple corrected SQL blocks (range filter, batched children, keyset, index)
